content.full_text:
October 5, 2000


Fred Aguiar
San Bernardino County Supervisor
P.O. Box 602
Ontario, CA 91762

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-00-192

Dear Mr. Aguiar:

This letter responds to your request for advice under the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ Since you are asking about general interpretation of definitions under the Act,
we are treating your request as one for informal assistance. (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(B).)
Informal assistance does not provide the immunity conferred by formal written advice.
(Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

QUESTION

Is Western Land Properties a commercial lending institution under the Act?

CONCLUSION

If Western Land Properties only makes short-term loans on occasions, it is not a
commercial lending institution for purposes of the Act.

FACTS

You recently purchased a new home. While your previous residence was still in
escrow, you acquired a short-term loan on your new residence through Western Land
Properties. You had not reported the loan through Western Land Properties believing it
to be a commercial lending institution. You are now concerned that your initial belief is
untrue and that you may need to amend your previously filed statement of economic

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 –
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 1-00-192
Page 2

interests to report the interim loan on your principal place of residence.² Western Land
Properties makes short-term loans on occasion.

ANALYSIS

The Act's disclosure provisions require public officials to disclose their financial
interests, specifically their sources of income. Income is defined as a payment received,
including but not limited to any salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds
from any sale, gift, including any gift of food or beverage, loan…and any community
property interest in the income of a spouse. (Section 82030(a).) Under Section
82030(b)(8), "income" does not include:

"Any loan or loans from a commercial lending institution which are made
in the lender's regular course of business on terms available to members of
the public without regard to official status if:

(A) Used to purchase, refinance the purchase of, or for improvement to,
the principal residence of the filer; or

(B) The balance owed does not exceed ten thousand dollars ($10,000)."

You have asked if Western Land Properties is a "commercial lending institution"
under Section 82030(b)(8). However, you have not provided us with any information on
Western Land Properties, or its practices, to enable us to provide a definitive conclusion.
However, you stated that Western Land Properties makes loans on occasions.

The term "commercial lending institution" is not defined in Section 82030 or
elsewhere in the Act, but the term has rarely led to uncertainty in practice. The question
was previously addressed in the Wilsey Advice Letter, No. A-84-023, where we
concluded that the term embraced an agricultural "Production Credit Association,"
organized as a nonprofit cooperative. In Wilsey, we found that a loan from an entity
would be excluded from the definition of "income" if the remaining statutory criteria
were satisfied. Those criteria are: (1) the institution regularly makes loans; (2) the loan
in question is made in the regular course of business, on terms available to the general
public, without regard to the official status of the borrower; and (3) under Section
82030(b)(8), the loan is either secured by the official's principal residence, or the balance
owed on the loan does not exceed $10,000.

Therefore, if Western Land Properties is an institution that regularly makes loans
and the loan to you was made in its regular course of business, on terms available to the
general public without regard to your official status, the loan meets the exception of
"income," provided the loan was secured by your principal residence. If this is the case,
it is not necessary for you to amend your most recently filed statement of economic

²The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC
Ops. 71, 77.)

File No. I-00-192
Page 3

interests. However, under the limited facts provided, it does not appear Western Land
Properties is a "commercial lending institution" as described above.

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel


By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Technical Assistance Division

CONCLUSION: If Western Land Properties only makes short-term loans on occasions, it is not a
commercial lending institution for purposes of the Act.
